inline_comments.message:
New line:
```
Maintenance release provides additional bugfixes. This Releasenote combines both Honolulu and Honolulu Maintenance Release data.
```
What should be put there? Is there any recommendation of codename/version for maintenance release?

I think it is fine
OOM has the list of the versions corresponding to the release, maintenance release,...
MultiCloud does not participate maintenance release. So it should be patch back to honolulu replease .

I do not think it is possible...you cannot move the OOM tags of an already released versions.
you can provide fixes as part of the branch or in a maintenance release but you cannot change an existing release, can you?

For multicloud participating to the maintenance release does not mean much..it is just indicating to take the 0.8.2 to OOM + update the doc (this patch)

If I understand the situation correctly, "take the 0.8.2 to OOM" is already done, and update the doc is what we are working on now (I guess we need to increase the release version with this patch), what else we need to do to participate to the maintenance release? Thanks

my understanding (but I am not the release manager)
you submitted the 0.8.2 in master then in OOM honolulu branch
so you automatically participate to the maintenance release

You updated the release note indicating the change of version
so for me, once this patch merged, it is OK.
Maybe I would have indicated the history (that can be retrieve through git history anyway)

************************

Bug Fixes
---------
- `MULTICLOUD-1295 <https://jira.onap.org/browse/MULTICLOUD-1295>`_
  MultiCloud k8s: Bug in Multicloud K8S Plugin Detemplating
- `MULTICLOUD-1338 <https://jira.onap.org/browse/MULTICLOUD-1338>`_
  MultiCloud k8s: Foreground delete policy prevents deletion of the pods
- `MULTICLOUD-1348 <https://jira.onap.org/browse/MULTICLOUD-1348>`_

Thanks for the clarification

@Konrad,  Please update the release note with the suggestion from Morgan. Thanks
